title: Glyphaea brevis
taxonomy: Glyphaea brevis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Lythraceae. It belongs to the order Myrtales and the family Lythraceae is also known as the loosestrife family. Further, Glyphaea brevis is classified at the rank of genus within the family Lythraceae. The classification of Glyphaea brevis is as follows: kingdom - Plantae, class - Magnoliopsida, order - Myrtales, family - Lythraceae, and genus - Glyphaea. The specific classification of Glyphaea brevis helps in understanding its evolutionary relationships with other plant species.

intro: Glyphaea brevis is a plant species belonging to the family Acanthaceae, commonly known as the 'short-stalked plant' due to its compact growth habit. Traditionally, the plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects that can aid in the treatment of various health conditions, such as skin infections and wounds. The bioactive constituents of Glyphaea brevis include glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic benefits.
